---
title: "Unidad 10: Laboratorio Práctico - Nginx en Producción"
subtitle: "Instalación, reverse proxy, SSL y hardening"
author: "Diego Saavedra"
date: "2026-03-31"
date-format: medium
---

# Laboratorio 10: Nginx en Producción

## Introducción

En este laboratorio vas a instalar Nginx, configurar server blocks, implementar reverse proxy, configurar SSL con Certbot, y aplicar hardening de seguridad. Vas a usar Docker para pruebas locales.

## Objetivos del Laboratorio

Al completar este laboratorio podrás:

1. Instalar y validar Nginx en Ubuntu Server
2. Configurar múltiples server blocks
3. Implementar reverse proxy a backend
4. Configurar SSL con Certbot
5. Aplicar hardening de seguridad

## Requisitos

- Máquina con Docker instalado
- Acceso a un VPS con dominio (para SSL real)
- Puertos 80, 443 accesibles

## Duración

**120 minutos**

---

## Parte 1: Instalación y Validación

### Ejercicio 1.1: Instalar Nginx

```bash
# Actualizar e instalar
$ sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y nginx nginx-extras
# <1>

# Ver estado
$ sudo systemctl status nginx --no-pager
● nginx.service - A high performance web server and a reverse proxy server
     Loaded: loaded (/lib/systemd/system/nginx.service; enabled)
     Active: active (running)
# <2>
```

1. **nginx-extras**: Módulos adicionales

### Ejercicio 1.2: Validar Puertos

```bash
# Ver puertos
$ sudo ss -lntp | grep nginx
LISTEN 0 511 0.0.0.0:80 0.0.0.0:* users:("nginx",pid=1234,fd=6)

# Probar respuesta
$ curl -I http://localhost
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Server: nginx/1.24.0
# <1>
```

### Ejercicio 1.3: Estructura de Archivos

```bash
# Ver estructura
$ ls -la /etc/nginx/

# Ver configuración principal
$ head -30 /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
```

---

## Parte 2: Server Blocks

### Ejercicio 2.1: Crear Estructura

```bash
# Crear directorios para dos sitios
$ sudo mkdir -p /var/www/site1/{public,logs}
$ sudo mkdir -p /var/www/site2/{public,logs}

# Crear contenido
$ echo '<!DOCTYPE html><h1>Site 1</h1>' | sudo tee /var/www/site1/public/index.html
$ echo '<!DOCTYPE html><h1>Site 2</h1>' | sudo tee /var/www/site2/public/index.html

# Permisos
$ sudo chown -R www-data:www-data /var/www/site*
# <1>
```

### Ejercicio 2.2: Crear Server Blocks

```bash
# Site 1
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/sites-available/site1.local <<'EOF'
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name site1.local;
    
    root /var/www/site1/public;
    index index.html;
    
    access_log /var/www/site1/logs/access.log;
    error_log /var/www/site1/logs/error.log;
    
    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ =404;
    }
}
EOF

# Site 2
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/sites-available/site2.local <<'EOF'
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name site2.local;
    
    root /var/www/site2/public;
    index index.html;
    
    access_log /var/www/site2/logs/access.log;
    error_log /var/www/site2/logs/error.log;
    
    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ =404;
    }
}
EOF
# <1>
```

### Ejercicio 2.3: Habilitar Sitios

```bash
# Crear symlinks
$ sudo ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/site1.local /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
$ sudo ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/site2.local /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/

# Remover default
$ sudo rm -f /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/default

# Validar y recargar
$ sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx

# Probar
$ curl -H 'Host: site1.local' http://localhost
<h1>Site 1</h1>

$ curl -H 'Host: site2.local' http://localhost
<h1>Site 2</h1>
# <1>
```

---

## Parte 3: Reverse Proxy

### Ejercicio 3.1: Backend Simple

```bash
# Crear backend falso con netcat o Python
$ timeout 300 python3 -m http.server 3000 --directory /tmp &
# <1>
```

1. **Backend**: Servidor simple en puerto 3000

### Ejercicio 3.2: Configurar Proxy

```bash
# Crear configuración de proxy
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/sites-available/api-proxy <<'EOF'
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name api.local;
    
    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
        
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    }
}
EOF

$ sudo ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/api-proxy /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
$ sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx

# Probar (si hay backend corriendo)
$ curl -H 'Host: api.local' http://localhost
# <1>
```

1. **Proxy**: Forward a backend

### Ejercicio 3.3: Docker como Backend

```bash
# Iniciar contenedor backend
$ docker run -d --name nginx-backend -p 3000:80 nginx:alpine

# Ver IP del contenedor
$ docker inspect nginx-backend -f '{{range .NetworkSettings.Networks}}{{.IPAddress}}{{end}}'

# Configurar proxy a Docker
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/sites-available/docker-proxy <<'EOF'
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name docker.local;
    
    location / {
        proxy_pass http://172.17.0.2:80;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
    }
}
EOF
# <1>
```

1. **Docker backend**: Nginx en contenedor como backend

---

## Parte 4: SSL con Certbot

### Ejercicio 4.1: Instalar Certbot

```bash
# Instalar
$ sudo apt install -y certbot python3-certbot-nginx

# Verificar
$ certbot --version
# <1>
```

### Ejercicio 4.2: Certificado Auto-firmado (Testing)

```bash
# Generar certificado
$ sudo openssl req -x509 -nodes -days 365 -newkey rsa:2048 \
    -keyout /etc/ssl/private/nginx-selfsigned.key \
    -out /etc/ssl/certs/nginx-selfsigned.crt \
    -subj "/C=US/ST=Test/L=Test/O=Test/CN=localhost"
# <1>

# Crear configuración SSL
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/sites-available/ssl-test <<'EOF'
server {
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    server_name ssl.local;
    
    ssl_certificate /etc/ssl/certs/nginx-selfsigned.crt;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/ssl/private/nginx-selfsigned.key;
    
    root /var/www/site1/public;
    index index.html;
    
    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ =404;
    }
}
EOF

$ sudo ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/ssl-test /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
$ sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx

# Probar
$ curl -k https://localhost -H 'Host: ssl.local'
<h1>Site 1</h1>
# <2>
```

1. **Self-signed**: Para testing
2. **-k**: Aceptar certificado no válido

### Ejercicio 4.3: Certbot con Dominio Real (Opcional)

```bash
# Obtener certificado (dominio real)
$ sudo certbot --nginx -d example.com -d www.example.com
# <1>

# Ver configuración
$ ls /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/
```

1. **Certbot**: Automático con Let's Encrypt

---

## Parte 5: Hardening

### Ejercicio 5.1: Ocultar Información

```bash
# Editar nginx.conf
$ sudo sed -i 's/server_tokens on;/server_tokens off;/' /etc/nginx/nginx.conf

# Recargar
$ sudo systemctl reload nginx

# Verificar
$ curl -I http://localhost | grep Server
Server: nginx
# <1>
```

### Ejercicio 5.2: Security Headers

```bash
# Crear snippet
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/snippets/security-headers.conf <<'EOF'
add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
add_header Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" always;
add_header X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block" always;
EOF

# Agregar a cada server block
$ sudo sed -i '/location \/ {/a\    include /etc/nginx/snippets/security-headers.conf;' /etc/nginx/sites-available/site1.local

# Validar y recargar
$ sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx

# Verificar
$ curl -I http://localhost -H 'Host: site1.local' | grep -i x-
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN
Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block
# <1>
```

### Ejercicio 5.3: Rate Limiting

```bash
# Agregar al http block
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/conf.d/ratelimit.conf <<'EOF'
limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=general:10M rate=10r/s;
EOF

# Agregar a location
$ sudo sed -i '/location \/ {/a\    limit_req zone=general burst=20 nodelay;' /etc/nginx/sites-available/site1.local

# Validar y recargar
$ sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx
```

---

## Parte 6: Docker para Pruebas

### Ejercicio 6.1: Nginx en Docker

```bash
# Crear red
$ docker network create lab-nginx-net

# Iniciar Nginx
$ docker run -d --name nginx-server \
    --network lab-nginx-net \
    -p 8080:80 \
    nginx:1.25
# <1>

# Ver
$ docker ps | grep nginx
# <2>
```

1. **Puerto 8080**: Mapeo local 8080 → container 80
2. **Docker Nginx**: Corriendo

### Ejercicio 6.2: Configuración Personalizada

```bash
# Copiar configuración
$ docker cp /etc/nginx/nginx.conf nginx-server:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf

# Recargar
$ docker exec nginx-server nginx -s reload
```

---

## Parte 7: VPS Real (Opcional)

### Ejercicio 7.1: Deploy a VPS

```bash
# Conectar
$ ssh usuario@vps "sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y nginx"

# Copiar configuración
$ scp /etc/nginx/sites-available/site1.local usuario@vps:/tmp/
$ scp /etc/nginx/snippets/security-headers.conf usuario@vps:/tmp/

# Aplicar en VPS
$ ssh usuario@vps "sudo cp /tmp/site1.local /etc/nginx/sites-available/ && \
    sudo cp /tmp/security-headers.conf /etc/nginx/snippets/ && \
    sudo ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/site1.local /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/ && \
    sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx"
```

---

## Verificación de Laboratorio

| Habilidad | Verificación |
|-----------|-------------|
| Nginx instalado | `systemctl status nginx` activo |
| Server blocks | `curl -H 'Host: site1.local' localhost` |
| Reverse proxy | `curl -H 'Host: api.local' localhost` |
| SSL | `curl -k https://localhost` |
| Hardening | `curl -I localhost \| grep X-Frame` |
| Rate limit | `for i in {1..15}; do curl -s -o /dev/null localhost; done` |

---

## Limpieza

```bash
# Detener contenedores
$ docker stop nginx-server nginx-backend
$ docker rm nginx-server nginx-backend
$ docker network rm lab-nginx-net

# En VPS
$ ssh usuario@vps "sudo rm /etc/nginx/sites-available/site1.local && sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx"
```

---

## Referencias

- [Nginx Official](https://nginx.org/)
- [Certbot](https://certbot.eff.org/)
- [Nginx Admin Guide](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx/admin-guide/)
